mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate over the prior three months above its trailing 2-year median level signals accelerating labor market deterioration that raises recession odds and risk premia, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days following such signals.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 2-year rolling median AND 3-month change in UNRATE > 0
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has risen sharply through 2008 into 6.1% by Sep-2008
failure_condition: In regimes where UNRATE exceeds its 2-year median and has risen over the prior 3 months, realized 20-day S&P 500 returns are non-negative in at least 60% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the average 20-day return is not significantly below zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines over the prior six months while the level of the rate remains below its 5-year median, this signals an aggressively accommodative policy stance that lowers discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in FEDFUNDS < 0 AND FEDFUNDS < 5-year rolling median
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has cut rates sharply through 2008 with FEDFUNDS around 1.8% by Sep-2008
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial stress
failure_condition: Across all episodes where FEDFUNDS is below its 5-year median and has declined over the prior 6 months, the S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases or the mean 60-day return is not higher than in non-signal periods with statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent three-month decline in industrial production index levels signals broad-based contraction in real activity and corporate output that depresses earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days once the third consecutive monthly decline is observed.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 3 consecutive months on a month-over-month basis
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has rolled over into late 2008 indicating industrial contraction
regime_hint: recession onset or deepening industrial downturn
failure_condition: In periods with at least three consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in at least 60% of triggered instances, or the mean 20-day return does not differ significantly from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal consumption growth, proxied by retail and food services sales, exhibits short-term momentum such that months with above-median real RSAFS growth relative to the prior year predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, reflecting stronger current demand and earnings resilience.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year growth in RSAFS minus year-over-year growth in CPIAUCSL > 0 and above its 3-year rolling median
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS levels remained relatively elevated into mid-2008 despite stress
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with still resilient consumption
failure_condition: When real RSAFS year-over-year growth exceeds its 3-year median and is positive, the S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases or the average 20-day return is not higher than in periods with weaker real RSAFS growth at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: In environments where the federal funds rate is falling but still above its trailing 2-year minimum, a further surprise cut below the 2-year minimum level signals severe macro stress and investor risk aversion that dominates discount rate benefits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days after the new low is registered.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS sets a new 2-year low relative to all observations in the prior 24 months
evidence_refs: fact:Aggressive emergency cuts in 2008 accompanied sharp equity drawdowns
regime_hint: crisis or panic-driven monetary easing episodes
failure_condition: On dates when FEDFUNDS reaches a new 2-year low, the S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mean 5-day return is not significantly below the unconditional 5-day mean at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in housing starts and retail sales over the prior six months signals broad household demand retrenchment that anticipates earnings downgrades and equity underperformance, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when both HOUST and RSAFS six-month changes are negative.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in HOUST < 0 AND 6-month change in RSAFS < 0
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST collapsed into 2008 while retail sales softened
regime_hint: housing-led demand downturn or consumer recession
failure_condition: During episodes where both HOUST and RSAFS are down over the prior 6 months, the S&P 500 delivers non-negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or the average 60-day return is not significantly below zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment momentum, measured by the three-month change in PAYEMS, acts as an always-on trend indicator such that positive three-month PAYEMS growth above its 5-year median corresponds to improving labor demand and stronger corporate revenues, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of additional triggers.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, periods of strong payroll growth align with equity strength
failure_condition: Across all history, when the 3-month change in PAYEMS is above its 5-year median, the S&P 500 fails to deliver positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of observations, or the rank correlation between the PAYEMS 3-month change and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP level is above its 5-year trend while unemployment is rising relative to its 2-year minimum, this combination signals a transition from late-cycle strength to early recession, where earnings expectations adjust downward faster than output, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days following such regime-transition signals.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 > 5-year rolling linear trend AND UNRATE > 2-year rolling minimum by at least 0.5 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:Real GDP was still elevated in early 2008 while unemployment began to rise
regime_hint: transition from late-cycle expansion to recession
failure_condition: In periods where GDPC1 exceeds its 5-year trend and UNRATE is at least 0.5 percentage points above its 2-year minimum, the S&P 500 shows non-negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of cases, or the mean 60-day return does not differ from zero at the 5% significance level.
